page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 248 GB of system memory, providing 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU, and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in single-threaded tasks and specialized workloads. The server achieves a strong Geekbench single-core score of 1933.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 47956.79, with strong results in CPU physics and image processing. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is also strong at 187.69 GB/sec. For AI workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions enable top-tier text generation speeds of 421.19 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, though performance drops to a poor 2.38 tokens/sec for large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Memory bandwidth is measured at 144.63 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 61.79 GB/sec for writes.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highmem-32 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis (achieving 39.05 million SET operations per second), static web serving (51.15 GB/sec throughput), and moderate-scale data processing or software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 248 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPUs. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Benchmarks show strong performance in single-threaded tasks, static web serving at 51.15 GB/sec, and small-model LLM text generation at 421.19 tokens/sec. However, large-model inference is a notable tradeoff, dropping to 2.38 tokens/sec. This instance is optimized for memory-heavy workloads, including in-memory databases, Redis caching, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 server with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It is optimized for memory-intensive workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and advanced Intel AMX matrix acceleration.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 248 GB RAM
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong single-core performance and 51.15 GB/sec static web serving throughput
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and web serving
